title: alchemilla coriacea subsp. inconcinna
intro: Alchemilla coriacea subsp. inconcinna is a sub-species of the Aconitella genus, commonly known as the alpine lady's mantle, a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and ascorbic acid. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. Alchemilla coriacea subsp. inconcinna can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall well-being and promote a balanced health.
